title: pilosella caespitosa subsp. colliniformis
intro: Pilosella caespitosa subsp. colliniformis, also known as Alpine Mouse-ear or Collared Mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit anti-inflammatory effects. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. Pilosella caespitosa subsp. colliniformis can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
